response: there is no direct built-in method in c# to get the exact memory size of a single object instance like tc, but you can use the system.runtime.interopservices.marshal class to estimate the size of the object's fields. this approach gives you an estimate rather than a perfect number because it does not account for the overhead of the garbage collector or the references to other objects. regarding your concern about clearing lists, remember that the garbage collection policy in .net is designed to reclaim memory automatically when objects are no longer referenced, so manually clearing a list is often unnecessary unless you need to free memory immediately for a specific reason. if you need deeper analysis in a multi-threaded environment, you might write a script to periodically capture memory snapshots or use tools that support threading, though this can be complex. interestingly, this problem is not unique to western programming languages, as many managed environments face similar challenges with memory visibility.
